host_version_seen: canonical wire-shape; corpus reflects the protocol surface, not the host build (no live capture yet — see README § When to update)

rationale.tools_list_must_include: These seven tool names are the agent-callable surface every host has shipped against since v0.51 (per `mcp_surface_split_test.go` working set). A new release breaking this list breaks every host in the matrix simultaneously.
rationale.search_response_shape: Anchors the FTS5-backed search path. The regex requirement proves the response is semantically useful (matching content was returned), not just that the envelope is well-formed.
rationale.architecture_response_shape: Anchors the cross-cutting orient-on-unfamiliar-area path; this tool failing post-release strands users on first contact.
rationale.error_envelope_forbidden_on_lines: MCP allows tool-call errors via `result.isError: true` or `error: {...}` at the JSON-RPC level. Either shape on the listed lines means the host's canonical flow does not work — release blocker by definition.
